mechanism_narrative: A persistently low or falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers discount rates and supports equity valuations, so S&P 500 forward returns are positive when policy is easing and inflation is stable. The signal is falsified if rate cuts occur alongside worsening growth and equities still decline over the next month.
failure_condition: If 20-day SPX forward returns are not positive in a majority of months when FEDFUNDS is falling or below its 6-month average, or if rank IC stays below 0.03 in a 3-year rolling window, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE above its recent trend indicates weakening household income and lower cyclical demand, which reduces expected corporate earnings and pushes S&P 500 returns negative over the next month. The signal is strongest when payroll growth is also soft.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered instances of UNRATE increases still deliver positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the sign of the effect flips positive in two consecutive 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in RSAFS combined with rising HOUST signals broad domestic demand and housing-led activity, which improves revenue expectations and supports positive equity performance over the next quarter. The mechanism fails when sales strengthen but housing starts collapse, implying a distribution mismatch rather than true demand expansion.
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction term between RSAFS and HOUST does not improve out-of-sample prediction versus either series alone, or if positive joint-growth episodes do not beat baseline SPX 60-day returns by at least 3 percentage points on average.

mechanism_narrative: A renewed upturn in INDPRO after a prior slowdown signals a manufacturing recovery, which lifts cyclical earnings expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next week. The effect is falsified if industrial production improves but equity returns remain negative during the same short-run window.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises for 2 consecutive months after a decline
failure_condition: Reject if 5-day SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of INDPRO rebound episodes, or if the conditional mean return is indistinguishable from zero across all post-decline rebounds.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation rises faster than FEDFUNDS, real policy rates become more negative, which eases financial conditions and supports a positive S&P 500 response over the next month. The signal is invalid if inflation surprises do not change the equity response once policy rates are already near zero.
failure_condition: Reject if episodes with inflation above the policy rate do not produce higher 20-day SPX returns than episodes with inflation below the policy rate, or if the interaction adds no predictive value versus CPIAUCSL alone.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in GDPC1 after prior growth signals an earnings recession and weaker aggregate demand, which should pressure equity prices lower over the next quarter. The pattern is falsified when GDP contraction is followed by stable or rising S&P 500 returns despite no offsetting easing in policy.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 declines versus the prior quarter
failure_condition: Reject if quarterly GDP declines are not associated with below-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if the signal’s hit rate remains below 45% across triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE identifies improving labor market breadth, which boosts consumer spending expectations and raises S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect fails when payroll gains are concentrated but unemployment does not improve.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-UNRATE condition does not outperform either series individually in walk-forward testing, or if positive labor-improvement episodes fail to beat the unconditional 20-day SPX return average by at least 2 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, a rising FEDFUNDS rate together with sticky CPIAUCSL implies worsening real rates and valuation pressure, which predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next week. The mechanism is falsified if equities rally despite simultaneous rate hikes and elevated inflation.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises for 2 consecutive meetings or months
failure_condition: Reject if tightening episodes produce positive 5-day SPX returns more than 50% of the time, or if the interaction between rising FEDFUNDS and high CPIAUCSL does not remain negative in rolling out-of-sample tests.
